title: Product Brief: Orvex Studio
verdict_summary: Live umbrella brief (not superseded/stub). It restates no canon FR/NFR ids of its own; its build obligations are the [RULED 2026-07-13]/[NEW] session decisions plus the named components it stitches. Nearly all are covered by the service packs/stories: task-first wizard builder, Orvex multi-factor rating, marketplace social + reputation ledger, private memories/per-use consent, chat-history import, persona demo-data onboarding, beads->staging capture edge, Prompt Composer/editor, six-surface E2E, contracts/lib/bridge foundations, and the outbound PII redactor middleware are all present. Two genuine BUILD gaps remain: (1) the headline OUTBOUND memory sync / cross-AI sync to vendors' native memories, flagged [NEW - not yet in canon] and explicitly IN scope, has no story anywhere; (2) the AI-privacy setup that verifies a connected assistant won't train on your data (distinct from PII screening and from private memories) has no home.

gaps.requirement: Outbound memory sync (cross-AI sync to native vendor memories) - the un-lock-in layer
gaps.quote: Outbound - memory sync out. Optionally, Orvex syncs your Memory to the native memories of the chat systems you use... Orvex is the master copy of you; every other AI's memory becomes a replica. Free tier [RULED 2026-07-13]... [NEW - not yet in canon] Per-vendor reality: Claude ships a native memory-backend adapter; ChatGPT is platform-blocked today (OpenAI exposes no memory API)... with MCP interop as the universal fallback path. ... In (this program): ... outbound memory sync (optional; free tier - zero cost to us)
gaps.why_uncovered: No story in the 575-title feature-map or any breakdown/*/plan.json references outbound memory sync, cross-AI sync, native memory adapters, a Claude memory-backend adapter, or MCP-interop memory push. The only 'outbound' hits are the ai-service PII redactor/secret-scrubber middleware and generic outbound api-key helpers - none push Memory out to third-party vendor memories. This is an explicitly IN-scope, [NEW] BUILD obligation (a shippable product surface, also listed as the 'knowledge-sync' acceptance surface), so it needs a story, not just a pack.
gaps.suggested_home: orvex-studio-ai or orvex-studio-mcp (outbound memory-sync capability: per-vendor native adapters + MCP-interop fallback) - and a billing/ai canon amendment to record the free-tier ruling
gaps.verdict: CONFIRMED - source page rgBOQh31p3 re-checked live (status: canonical, updated 2026-07-13, not superseded); the executive summary and Scope section both restate 'outbound memory sync' as an in-scope, free-tier headline promise verbatim, so this is not a stale/dropped line. Re-searched feature-map.txt (575 titles) and every breakdown/*/plan.json covers[] for memory-sync/native-adapter/cross-AI/MCP-interop terms: the only real hit is workgraph E7-S4 'Claude memory-tool backend (memory_20250818 /memories file ops)' (covers FR-MEM27) plus workgraph E7-S3 'KG interop adapter' (FR-MEM16). Read both bodies: E7-S4's own Context section states explicitly 'this is agent-state, distinct from the user-memory product' - it backs Anthropic's memory_20250818 tool for platform-run Claude Code delivery AGENTS via the internal workgraph store, not the end-user-facing Orvex Memory product (api/ai's Memory, Private-Memory guard, etc.) described in this brief. My own memory index independently notes 'workgraph≠memory' as a standing gotcha. A companion gap-hunt (gaps-3z78laG6dB.json, the addendum) claimed 'zero gaps' by mapping outbound-sync onto FR-MEM27+FR-MEM16, but that conflates the two distinct memory systems and is contradicted by census-brief.json's own line item, which independently flags this exact feature as a PARTIAL GAP for the same reason (no story orchestrates active outbound push-sync of the user-facing Memory product to vendor native memories - conflict policy, private-memory exclusion, per-vendor adapter selection all unowned). No pack-certification issue (ENG-2091..2110 are contract-tag dispatch gates on already-filed stories, not story sources) and no relation to the browser-extension pair (ENG-2689/2690, unrelated F1 scope) closes this. Genuine unstoried BUILD gap on the user-facing product.

gaps.requirement: AI-privacy setup - verify the connected assistant won't train on your data
gaps.quote: AI-privacy setup verifies the connected assistant won't train on your data - distinct from private memories, which gate Orvex's own enrichment.
gaps.why_uncovered: Covered stories provide the outbound PII redactor/secret-scrubber middleware (the Personal-Data Guard) and the private-memory/per-use-consent guard, but nothing implements a setup/verification step that checks a connected third-party assistant's training-opt-out posture. The brief explicitly calls this 'distinct' from both. No plan.json references ai-privacy setup or won't-train verification (only workgraph's unrelated 'no-train').
gaps.suggested_home: orvex-studio-ai (connection/onboarding config) or the identity/console connection-setup surface, as part of the connected-assistant setup flow
gaps.verdict: CONFIRMED - source page live canon (see gap 1 verdict). The quoted sentence is real, explicit, and self-distinguishing ('distinct from private memories, which gate Orvex's own enrichment'), so it names a discrete obligation rather than loose prose. Re-searched: (a) ai E5-S4 'PII redactor + secret scrubber outbound middleware' - screens content going out, never checks the receiving vendor's training/retention posture; (b) ui E7-S2 'Connections & MCP setup (FR-UI25)' - covers Orvex-side token issuance for AI AGENTS connecting INTO Orvex, the opposite direction, with no training-opt-out verification AC; (c) api's Private-Memory guard + per-use consent - explicitly the OTHER thing the brief distinguishes this from; (d) the only 'train' hits repo-wide are workgraph E5-S4's 'no-train / EU-resident / zero-retention orvex-studio-ai provider config' (NFR-MEM7) - Orvex's own posture toward its OWN model provider for internal janitor calls, not a user-facing verification of a THIRD-PARTY connected assistant's training behavior. No plan.json covers[] anywhere references ai-privacy setup, training-opt-out verification, or won't-train confirmation for a connected assistant. Not owned by any pack-certification issue or by ENG-2689/2690. Genuine unstoried BUILD gap (a real product/trust feature, not process or ops).
